author: Carolann Wheelis
review: Absolutely do not rent from this company. At first they are communicative and responsive, but once you read the lease ALL red flags and gut feelings shouldnt be ignored. Once you move in its nearly impossible to get ahold of someone and

ownerResponse.text: RPM follows all Washington State Tenant/Landlord laws. In all leases that we draft, a tenant agrees to return the property in the same condition it was given to them, minus normal wear and tear. We document in writing and with pictures the condition of the property before a tenant moves in and after a tenant moves out. We are sorry that we could not come to a happy resolution for you.

author: nelstheviking
review: We used this company to manage our house, and had many bad experiences. They were always extremely difficult to get a hold of and wouldn't answer or return calls or get back to us with information we wanted. At times they charged us

ownerResponse.text:
Nels, thank you for your feedback regarding the rental property we have been managing for you for over 3 years.  RPM takes this kind of feedback very seriously and will be reaching out to you to resolve your concerns. At RPM we always get back to clients and tenants within 24 hours of any phone call or email correspondence. We are transparent on our maintenance fees and offer fair yet competitive repair estimates and prices. A detailed description of all maintenance work performed is found on your monthly online statements. In reviewing the repair history at your property I see a couple of emergency water leaks that were fixed expeditiously to prevent further property damage. As your property manager, I assure you, prevention of further damage to your property that water leaks can so easily cause, was our top priority through those repairs. Our communication history shows we kept you posted through the entirety of the emergency water leak repair process. One of those bills was over the $300 pre-approved amount, we informed you and received permission from you to do the work. However, our management contract grants us the authority to act in your best interest in the case of emergency situations such as water, fire, flood, etc.

Regarding the eviction of the tenant that stopped paying rent. Unfortunately, once the lease was signed, we later found out the approved tenant did not move in but instead had moved in his adult daughter, that would not have qualified.  Shortly after the lease started we discovered this and then the rent stopped coming in so we started the eviction process. We walked you through every step of the way. Once the Writ of Restitution was performed, we were at the property per your request when the Sheriff physically removed the resident. Our team here at RPM then removed all the residents property (couches, bed frames, mattresses, dishes etc) from inside the house onto the lawn. We updated you with photos. We would be happy to discuss this further. Thank you.

author: W01278 MGR4
review:
My wife and I have worked with Aaron and Lisa at RPMNPS for a year.  It has been a seamless experience for a new property owner.  Their analysis was spot on, and look forward to working with them for years to come.
Thanks for everything. Adam

ownerResponse.text: Adam, Thank you for your kind words. We appreciate you as a Client and will treat you as we would want to be treated.

review: Real Property Management is the only property management company that I refer my clients to. They are quick to respond, and are always on top of things. They are competitive with their rents, and are always putting their clients first.

author: James Robison
review: Be sure to read your lease this company is the worst property management company I have ever dealt with with moving in to moving out the required professional cleaning of a unit. They do nothing on their end, take care of nothing I don't

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the feedback.  We are sorry our interaction was not a positive one.  In this case, we were not the active property manager over the past year as this was a Lease-only Agreement with the property owner.  Our role was limited to helping with preparing the property for move-in and tenant placement only. This move-in preparation includes professional cleaning, which was done with receipt kept on file, prior to you moving in. When we met with you to sign the lease we reviewed all these terms with you in detail and all parties agreed to them and signed. Which includes the tenants having the property professionally cleaned upon vacating and to provide receipt. Again we are sorry for your experience and would be happy to discuss further.
